ACCOUNT_NAME.description: The recipient's full legal name as it appears on their bank account. Must match the bank's records exactly or the transfer will be rejected or returned.

ACCOUNT_NUM.description: The recipient's bank account number. Format varies by country — can be local account number, BBAN, or similar national identifier. For some countries this field also accepts IBAN.

IBAN.description: International Bank Account Number. A standardized alphanumeric code (up to 34 characters) that uniquely identifies a bank account across borders. Starts with the 2-letter country code, then check digits, then the basic account number. Used in 70+ countries (all EU plus AE, IL, JO, KW, PK, QA, SA, TR and others).

IBAN_OR_ACCOUNT_NUM.description: A hybrid field that accepts either an IBAN or a local account number. Used for countries that are in IBAN-partial adoption — some banks have IBAN, others don't. The system auto-detects which you entered.

NAT_CLEAR_CODE.description: The country-specific routing code that identifies the recipient's bank branch. The label and format change per country — Xe uses ONE field ID (NAT_CLEAR_CODE) with different country labels.

IN.label: IFSC
IN.description: 11-character Indian Financial System Code that identifies the bank branch. First 4 chars = bank, 5th = 0, last 6 = branch.

GB.label: Sort Code
GB.description: 6-digit UK sort code identifying the bank and branch, usually written XX-XX-XX.

US.label: ABA / Routing Number
US.description: 9-digit American Bankers Association routing number for US banks (used for ACH and wire transfers).

AU.label: BSB Number
AU.description: 6-digit Bank State Branch code identifying Australian bank branches.

CA.label: Bank Code & Transit
CA.description: 8-digit Canadian routing code: 3-digit institution number + 5-digit transit (branch) number.

HK.label: Hong Kong Bank Code
HK.description: Hong Kong clearing code — 3-digit bank code + 3-digit branch code.

PR.label: Routing Number
PR.description: 9-digit ABA routing number (Puerto Rico uses US system).

AS.label: Routing Number
AS.description: US ABA routing number (American Samoa uses US system).

GU.label: Routing Number
GU.description: US ABA routing number (Guam uses US system).

FM.label: Routing Number
FM.description: US ABA routing number (Micronesia uses US system).

MP.label: Routing Number
MP.description: US ABA routing number (Northern Mariana Islands uses US system).

VI.label: Routing Number
VI.description: US ABA routing number (US Virgin Islands uses US system).

BANK_BIC.description: Bank Identifier Code (also called SWIFT code). 8 or 11 characters identifying the recipient's bank globally. Used for international wires. In many countries Xe auto-fills this from the IBAN or account number — it appears as a read-only confirmation of which bank detected.

BANK_NAME.description: The name of the recipient's bank. In most corridors this is auto-filled by Xe after the IBAN, BIC, or clearing code is entered — users see it as a confirmation, not an input. Auto-population prevents user error.

BANK_BRANCH_NUMBER.description: Additional branch identifier required in specific corridors. For Bangladesh, this is a branch code required alongside the account number and bank name.

BANK_ACCOUNT_TYPE_CODE.description: Whether the account is Checking or Savings. Required in several LATAM corridors where bank routing differs by account type.

BANK_ADDRESS_LINE1.description: Street address of the recipient's bank branch. Usually optional, required only in a few corridors for compliance / wire routing purposes.

BANK_ADDRESS_LINE2.description: Second line of the bank's street address (e.g., suite, floor).

ADDRESS_LINE1.description: The recipient's street address, line 1. This is their home address, not the bank's. Required in most corridors for compliance. PO boxes are rejected.

ADDRESS_LINE2.description: Second line of the recipient's address (e.g., apartment, suite). Optional almost everywhere.

ADDRESS_COUNTRY_CODE.description: The country where the recipient lives. Auto-set by Xe based on the destination country — users don't see this as an input field.

RECIPIENT_CITY.description: The city where the recipient lives. Required in most corridors.

RECIPIENT_STATE.description: The state, province, or equivalent administrative region. Uses a predefined dropdown — only required in countries where the state affects banking/compliance: AU, CA, CN, MX, US.

RECIPIENT_POST_CODE.description: Recipient's postal code. Mandatory only in ~10 specific corridors (including AU, CA, GB, MX, NZ); optional elsewhere.

RECIPIENT_PHONE.description: Recipient's phone number. Required in 12 corridors — mostly LATAM (BR, AR, CL, CO, PE, GT, DO) plus KR, TR, PL, ZA, EG — for fraud prevention and local compliance.

EMAIL.description: Recipient's email address. Required in 5 specific corridors, optional elsewhere.

RECIPIENT_ID_NUMBER.description: Recipient's national ID / government-issued identifier. The type is chosen via ID_NUMBER_TYPE_CODE dropdown. Required in 4 corridors: PK (CNIC), CL (RUT), PE (DNI), DO.

ID_NUMBER_TYPE_CODE.description: Dropdown specifying which type of ID document the recipient provides. Different countries accept different ID types.

PK.label: ID Type
PK.description: Pakistan accepts: CNIC (Computerized National ID Card) — 13 digits; NICOP (NID for Overseas Pakistanis); Passport; Driving License; Residence Permit.

CL.label: ID Type
CL.description: Chile accepts: RUT (Rol Único Tributario) — standard Chilean tax ID.

PE.label: ID Type
PE.description: Peru accepts: DNI (Documento Nacional de Identidad), CE (Carnet de Extranjería), Passport.

DO.label: ID Type
DO.description: Dominican Republic accepts: Cédula (national ID), Passport.

RECIPIENT_TAX_NUMBER.description: Recipient's tax/fiscal identifier. Required in 5 LATAM corridors.

BR.label: CPF / CNPJ
BR.description: Brazilian tax ID. 11 digits for individuals (CPF — Cadastro de Pessoas Físicas) or 14 digits for businesses (CNPJ).

AR.label: CUIL / CUIT
AR.description: Argentine tax ID. 11 digits for individuals (CUIL) or businesses (CUIT).

CO.label: NIT / Cédula
CO.description: Colombian tax ID — NIT for businesses, Cédula (national ID) for individuals.

DO.label: RNC
DO.description: Dominican Republic tax ID (Registro Nacional de Contribuyentes).

INDUSTRY_TYPE.description: Business/industry classification code. Required only in Oman (OM) — used for sanctions screening.

CA_TRANSIT_INSTITUTION.description: 3-digit Canadian institution number that identifies the bank. First half of the Canadian routing code, combined with the transit (branch) number.

CA_TRANSIT_BRANCH.description: 5-digit Canadian transit number identifying the specific branch. Second half of the Canadian routing code. Xe splits what banks show as an 8-digit NAT_CLEAR_CODE into these two composite inputs in the UI.

NICKNAME.description: A label the sender can set to recognize this recipient in their list (e.g., 'Mum', 'Flat rent'). Optional everywhere, Xe-only — not sent to the bank.

OTHER_PARTY_REF.description: Free-text reference that appears on the recipient's bank statement. Optional. Useful for invoices, rent payments, etc.

IS_BUSINESS.description: Whether the recipient is a business (company) vs individual. Auto-set based on the form flow — not shown to users as an input.

INT_BANK_NAME.description: Name of a correspondent (intermediary) bank used to route SWIFT payments in some corridors. Always optional — for sophisticated users who know their bank requires a specific intermediary.

INT_BANK_BIC.description: SWIFT/BIC code of the intermediary bank. Always optional.

INT_BANK_NAT_CLEAR_CODE.description: National clearing code of the intermediary bank. Always optional.

INT_BANK_IBAN_OR_ACCOUNT_NUM.description: Intermediary bank's account number or IBAN. Always optional.

INT_BANK_ADDRESS_LINE1.description: Street address of the intermediary bank. Always optional.

INT_BANK_ADDRESS_LINE2.description: Second line of intermediary bank address. Always optional.

INT_BANK_ADDRESS_LINE3.description: Third line of intermediary bank address. Always optional.

INT_BANK_ADDRESS_COUNTRY_CODE.description: Country where the intermediary bank is located. Always optional.
